boardQuestion: Where is one model provider shaping too much of the product and delivery roadmap?
concentrationNarrative: One vendor is absorbing too much of the model-layer decision surface.
operatingReality: Prompt behavior, eval baselines, and customer-facing product expectations still revolve around one foundation model contract.
riskHeadline: Model strategy is moving faster than vendor diversification.
concentrationSignal: Critical routes still assume one provider's latency, tool semantics, and release cadence.
blockingIssue: The fallback model path is not strong enough to absorb a commercial or reliability shock.
headline: One model vendor is carrying too much of the AI control plane.
nextMove: Stand up one board-visible fallback path for every customer-facing model workflow above the premium margin threshold.

lane: Cloud AI bundle and hosting estate
boardQuestion: Which vendor bundle is becoming sticky because compute, identity, and AI workloads are being bought together?
concentrationNarrative: Convenience is drifting into stack-level dependency.
operatingReality: Inference, auth, storage, and telemetry are still packaged closely enough that exit math looks heavier every quarter.
riskHeadline: Platform leverage is improving while switching leverage is getting worse.
concentrationSignal: The same provider keeps winning adjacent layers because procurement and technical handoffs reward the bundle.
blockingIssue: The team lacks one clean isolation boundary for moving AI workloads without pulling infra and identity along with them.
opportunityMoves: separate identity from inference renewal windows
headline: The cloud AI bundle is getting too sticky.
nextMove: Force one renewal packet that prices AI, cloud, and identity together and separately before the next contract cycle.

boardQuestion: Which vendor relationship is creating hidden compliance dependency because policy, logging, and audit workflows depend on one trust stack?
concentrationNarrative: Compliance packaging is reusable, but still too dependent on one external proof shape.
operatingReality: Buyers can read the trust packet, though the underlying evidence model still assumes one vendor's semantics too often.
riskHeadline: Proof portability is weaker than the public trust story suggests.
concentrationSignal: Policy exports, audit evidence, and disclosure packets still map best to one provider's control framework.
blockingIssue: The estate lacks one portable control vocabulary that survives outside the current trust vendor contract.
headline: Compliance proof is still too dependent on one vendor's shape.
nextMove: Publish one vendor-neutral evidence index and force every trust packet to resolve through it first.

lane: Data pipelines and model fine-tuning estate
boardQuestion: Where is vendor concentration getting worse because training data, prompts, or evaluations are hard to move out cleanly?
concentrationNarrative: The estate can grow, but it cannot pivot cheaply enough yet.
operatingReality: Embeddings, eval traces, prompt assets, and fine-tuning assumptions still sit too close to one provider's stack.
riskHeadline: Portability remains weaker than procurement leverage.
concentrationSignal: The migration packet still requires too much format cleanup and too much model-specific adaptation work.
blockingIssue: There is no one durable export contract for prompts, evals, and supporting data flows.
opportunityMoves: dry-run one migration path every quarter
headline: Data portability is still too weak for comfortable vendor leverage.
nextMove: Create one provider-neutral export bundle for prompts, evals, and tuning traces before the next major vendor negotiation.

boardQuestion: Which AI supplier is gaining pricing power because revenue workflows are too dependent on one experience layer?
concentrationNarrative: Commercial upside is real, but pricing leverage is uneven.
operatingReality: Revenue workflows now assume one supplier's output quality in ways that reduce negotiation flexibility.
riskHeadline: Margin quality is improving while supplier leverage is slipping.
concentrationSignal: Growth workflows keep adding higher-value use cases faster than multi-vendor negotiating leverage is improving.
blockingIssue: The commercial team still lacks one clear downgrade or alternate-provider story for key AI-enhanced experiences.
opportunityMoves: tie usage growth to pricing tiers
negotiate with a visible fallback story
headline: Revenue AI is creating pricing-power exposure.
nextMove: Attach one alternate-provider or degraded-mode plan to every revenue-critical AI workflow above the margin review threshold.

boardQuestion: Which leadership-facing lane still looks diversified on paper but lacks enough real multi-vendor readiness to prove it?
concentrationNarrative: The strategy sounds diversified faster than the implementation proves it.
operatingReality: The portfolio references many vendors, but too few surfaces show hardened alternate-path readiness.
riskHeadline: Narrative diversification is ahead of operational diversification.
concentrationSignal: Too many leadership surfaces can describe supplier options but cannot yet demonstrate clean substitution under pressure.
blockingIssue: There is no one recurring drill packet that proves a concentrated lane can survive provider disruption or repricing.
headline: The diversification story still needs more proof.
nextMove: Add one quarterly substitution drill and one confidence score to every concentrated executive-intelligence lane.
